item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Author has specified images are released under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: Re: Images won't load on mobile.
item.content:encoded: I attempted a fix. Strange. I'll see what I can do.

item.title: Images won't load on mobile.
item.content:encoded: Someone else already said this, but the images aren't loading in at all anymore. Is anyone else having this problem?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The images are not working properly on mobile. When tapped, they become clear at first but then they become slightly blurry. It makes it hard to read some of the clip-art and next to impossible to read Clippy’s text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but it definitely seems to be lacking a "why". I figured that there would be some measure of investigation into Microsoft, Word and Clippy's creation mentioned at least as a footnote that'd read "nothing suspicious was found" if that's what you're going for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This might come off as overly harsh or out of line and if it does I apologize. This is definitely just my opinion and clearly there is something here hooking other people. That having been said, I think this entry is all hat and no cattle.

You're hooking into concepts that might be weighty in essence, but the avatars you're using are superficial and kind of pathetic. I'm old enough to remember Bonzibuddy. I remember friends having to clean it off of their grandparents' computers (my grandmother had needed similar help in the more distant past, but she was, perhaps fortunately, dead by this particular vintage of human parasitism masquerading as software). Anyway, it's not like the thing was hard to defeat after a week of exposure to raw human revulsion. And that's kind of the thing, right? You're trading on a species of virus that humans created, and not particularly ingenious humans either. That's not scary, it's irritating. If you want me to be irritated, cool, we could go with that concept, but I feel like you're reaching for something more visceral (especially with the last terminal log).

I could, theoretically, see something in this idea: there is something wrong with people, that they want to do this to one another, that anonymity abets psychopathy; I could even see this taken in a sort of Scarlet King direction, that some force outside of what we consider the real is injecting itself into our societies through information exchange and Shannon entropy. This really isn't either, though.

What this is, both in-universe and in-reality, is a kind of fright mask. It's the kind that you buy from the pop up store in the mall, the kind that trades on a very niche nostalgia and can't conjure enough real horror to mean anything in and of itself.

The faux-Lovecraftian existential dread that I think you're going for can't be supported by the material provided. If you want to sell this, for me at least, you'll have to hook deeper. You want me to believe that bonzibuddy was the extension of a horror from outside time and space? Okay, I'm game. Why is it threatening me in human terms, though, through its dialogue? Because right now it sounds petty, and insipid. The Foundation in most described canons can handle this without working up a sweat, and you've given no reason for me to believe otherwise. Hell, if this thing showed up on my PC, I'd be vaguely amused by the author's schtick and then obliterate it, because it's not hard to do that anymore. We evolve, and we're already past the point where this particular brand of human malevolence was a concern, much less a believable cosmic threat.

If all you're reaching for is a vaguely amusing early aughts in-joke, fine. I can get that. It didn't land with me, but maybe others find it funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much thanks for the crit! I’ll keep it in mind for when I write a compulsionary effect next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh lord it's the revenge of 90's share/spyware!!! I remember when those things were ubiquitous and all anyone ever wanted to do was bend Clippy into a shape that he couldn't recover from.

And then we found out Bonzibuddy was actually spyware and suddenly no one wanted "assistants" anymore (not that we wanted the damn things to begin with). +1 for nostalgia you bad bad person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The weirdness here just seems incoherent — there's no consistent themes, no particularly engaging or novel concepts, and the whole thing just builds up to a punchline that feels (to me, anyway) extremely derivative of The Great Hippo's "Skippy" character. It's literally just a thing that compels you to do things; the strangeness of the things it makes you do is its only saving grace, and even that is far over the line into just… lolrandom wackiness.

Sorry, but there's very little here I like; it's a poor concept held aloft entirely by the meme-iness of wordart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As of NOW
devout yourself to
themoisture

I love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Revolutions all the time, people changing the world with crime. With violence, and anger, hunger, whatever. I find that ridiculous, if not meticulous. I do my best to guard my own premises.

I… I don't know what to say about this. This was a joke on Discord. I didn't mean for this to be real. ~~God~~ Clippy save me now.

All images were created by me.
